# copy files for robot image (without creating our own)
# check container is empty first
root@ubuntu:~/_dev/62405_logback/oom/kubernetes# kubectl exec -it  onap-robot-7c84f54558-vfdgq  -n onap bash
root@onap-robot-7c84f54558-vfdgq:/# ls
bin   dev  home  lib64  mnt  proc  run   share  sys  usr
boot  etc  lib   media  opt  root  sbin  srv    tmp  var

root@ubuntu:~/_dev/62405_logback/testsuite# kubectl cp robot/resources/pomba_interface.robot  onap-robot-7c84f54558-vfdgq: -n onap  
root@ubuntu:~/_dev/62405_logback/testsuite# kubectl cp robot/testsuites/health-check.robot  onap-robot-7c84f54558-vfdgq: -n onap
# check for files in the robot container
root@onap-robot-7c84f54558-vfdgq:/# ls
bin   etc                 lib    mnt                    proc  sbin   sys  var
boot  health-check.robot  lib64  opt                    root  share  tmp
dev   home                media  pomba_interface.robot  run   srv    usr
# move them
root@onap-robot-7c84f54558-tkmb2:/# cp pomba_interface.robot /var/opt/OpenECOMP_ETE/robot/resources/
root@onap-robot-7c84f54558-tkmb2:/# cp health-check.robot /var/opt/OpenECOMP_ETE/robot/testsuites/

#exit container, run healthcheck
